title: Meet contrast thresholds for text and UI
description: Ensure text and meaningful UI elements meet WCAG contrast ratios and do not convey information by color alone.
body: Use when choosing or reviewing colors for text, icons, focus rings, and state indicators. Do not signal state (error, success, required) with color alone, and do not assume a brand palette passes. Actions: measure foreground/background contrast (target 4.5:1 for normal text, 3:1 for large text and UI components), add a non-color cue (icon, text, underline), and record measured ratios. Validation signals include a contrast tool report at the failing pairs and a check under a color-blindness simulator. Risk boundary: low contrast and color-only meaning exclude low-vision and color-blind users; full conformance still requires manual review beyond automated ratio checks.
